;<span id="Post-lapse_stability">post-lapse stability</span> (PLS)
:[[Glossary:Stability|stability]] of an [[Glossary:Item|item]] after a [[Glossary:Repetition|repetition]] that scored a failing [[Glossary:Grade|grade]]. '''Post-lapse stability''' can be interpreted as the ''[[Glossary:Optimum_interval|optimum interval]] after forgetting'' and rarely goes beyond mere days. On occasion, an [[Glossary:Item|item]] might be forgotten by "accident" and still remembered years after the [[Glossary:Lapse|lapse]], however, statistically this is an insignificant phenomenon as visualized by '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Toolkit menu#Statistics|Statistics]] : [[Analysis]] : [[Analysis#Graphs|Graphs]] : [[Analysis#First interval|First interval]]''' and '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Toolkit menu#Memory|Memory]] : [[Memory graphs (4D)|4D Graphs]] : First interval'''. Due to its easy interpretation as interval, '''post-lapse stability''' is also called '''post-lapse interval'''. See more: [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-17#Post-lapse_stability SuperMemo Algorithm]
